title: xanthophytum nitens
intro: Xanthophytum nitens is a species of flowering plant in the family Clusiaceae, commonly known as the yellow gum or yellow sap tree due to its yellow sap. Native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia, this plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The extracts of Xanthophytum nitens have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, as well as promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of the plant are commonly used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
